question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user determine whether a specific storage key exists in a hook by supplying that key in HookStorageRequest.keys and observing whether getHookStorage() returns a result, effectively probing hook state without owner or signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user use getHookStorageChange() to observe the exact consensus_timestamp at which a hook's storage was last modified, enabling timing-based attacks against authorization flows without requiring any signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that entityService.lookup() resolves any EntityIdParameter without caller verification, allowing the attacker to enumerate hooks for system-level or privileged accounts without those accounts' signer approval?
